data.title: Multi-agent coordination primitives: ticket claim and inter-agent messages
data.description:
## Context

Multi-agent DSO sessions today coordinate via opaque `[LOCK]` placeholder tickets created by `agent-batch-lifecycle.sh:436` — an ad hoc convention that provides no typed semantics for claiming work, signaling availability, or passing messages between agents. The beads project (steveyegge/beads) provides first-class `claim`, `ready`, and inter-agent messaging primitives over its event log. This epic adds equivalent DSO-native primitives built on the existing event-sourced log — not a parallel mechanism — so all coordination is auditable through the same reducer pipeline as regular ticket state.

Agent-to-agent messages are distinct from COMMENT events in that they have a sender, a recipient (agent role or specific claim), a read state, and can gate agent behavior until delivery.

## Success Criteria
- `ticket claim <id>` performs an atomic compare-and-swap on the ticket assignee field: succeeds only if assignee is currently unset or held by the caller; exits non-zero with a clear error otherwise.
- Existing `[LOCK]` placeholder tickets replaced with typed `Agent:Coordination`-reporter tickets or a distinct CLAIM event type; `agent-batch-lifecycle.sh` migrated to the new mechanism.
- New MESSAGE event type (or typed COMMENT subtype) records sender, recipient, body, and read state; reducer computes unread queue per agent.
- `ticket messages <id> [--unread]` prints messages addressed to the calling agent; `ticket messages --ack <message-uuid>` marks read.
- Stale-claim TTL: claims older than a configurable threshold (default 4 hours) are auto-released on next `ticket claim` attempt with an audit event.
- Sprint orchestration integrates the new primitives: dispatched sub-agents claim their ticket on start, emit a completion MESSAGE on finish, and release the claim on landing.

## Approach
Extend the reducer schema with a `claim` field (holder + timestamp) and an unread-messages queue. Add `ticket-claim.sh` and `ticket-messages.sh` subcommands implemented as thin shims over a shared Python library (leverages epic 78fc-3858 sourceable lib). Introduce a CLAIM event type with CAS semantics (rejects if current holder != expected). Introduce a MESSAGE event type indexed by recipient. Migrate `agent-batch-lifecycle.sh` atomically.

## Dependencies
- Depends on Epic 1 (3e74-56da): ID hardening and concurrent-merge safety must land first before CAS semantics are robust.
- Coordinates with Epic 4 (reporter/resolution): MESSAGE sender field uses the same reporter enum.

## Out of Scope
- Cross-repo agent coordination
- Real-time notifications (push) — messages are pull-only
- Priority queues for messages
